message:
fix(connectors): add InvalidInput error variant and migrate 400-status call sites

Introduce a dedicated InvalidInput(String) variant on each connector's
error enum and migrate call sites that were previously abusing the
generic Api { status_code: 400, .. } envelope for input-validation
failures.

Each connector's Error impl now maps InvalidInput to
FcpError::InvalidRequest { code: 1005, message: .. }, giving callers a
semantically correct error taxonomy — validation failures no longer
masquerade as HTTP API errors and get the proper InvalidRequest code
instead of the catch-all Api variant.

Scope (17 connectors, 34 files):
- 1password, bitbucket, bitwarden, datadog, dropbox, duckdb, hubspot,
  intercom, linkedin, make, metabase, mongodb, n8n, reddit, retool,
  todoist: each connector/src/error.rs gains InvalidInput(String) +
  mapping; each connector/src/connector.rs (plus client.rs where
  relevant — duckdb, intercom, todoist) rewrites ~1-3 call sites that
  built Api { status_code: 400, .. } to instead return
  Error::InvalidInput(...) with a clear message

Continues the error-taxonomy initiative from recent commit
"fix(connectors): FCP error taxonomy and path sanitization".
No behavioral change for successful paths; errors remain returned,
just with more accurate classification.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -444,10 +444,7 @@ fn require_str<'a>(input: &'a serde_json::Value, field: &str) -> Result<&'a str,
     input
         .get(field)
         .and_then(serde_json::Value::as_str)
-        .ok_or_else(|| OnePasswordError::Api {
-            status_code: 400,
-            message: format!("Missing required field: {field}"),
-        })
+        .ok_or_else(|| OnePasswordError::InvalidInput(format!("Missing required field: {field}")))
 }
